# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: SteamVR
- Steam AppID of the game: 250820

## System Information
- GPU: AMD RX 5700 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.1.2
- Kernel version: 5.12.10-300.fc34.x86_64
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/):
- Proton version: Experimental (Jun 19 2021)

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->
[steam-250820.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6677616/steam-250820.log)
First few lines:
```

654.682:0030:00ac:trace:seh:NtQueryInformationThread (0xfffffffffffffffe,12,0x386fe2c,4,(nil))
654.683:00dc:00f8:trace:seh:NtQueryInformationThread (0xfffffffffffffffe,12,0x202fe2c,4,(nil))

654.683:005c:0070:trace:seh:NtQueryInformationThread (0xfffffffffffffffe,12,0x1a3fe2c,4,(nil))
```

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
SteamVR does not launch on Fedora 34. See specs above. Competely out of the box fresh install.

## Reproduction
1. Install Fedora 34
2. Install Steam
3. Install SteamVR for Windows (Compatibility -> Force Steam Play (Proton))
4. Run SteamVR

following_discussion.discussion_body: Hello @l33tllama, we're using one issue report per unofficially supported game title (in this case a utility), so I've gone ahead and transferred this issue report to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/2595#issuecomment-864091142.

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Quake Champions
- Steam AppID of the game: 611500

## System Information
- GPU: GTX1650
- Driver/LLVM version: NVIDIA 510.54
- Kernel version: 5.15.0-23-generic
- Link to full system information report as [[Gist](https://gist.github.com/virnik0/3a1ac67fd099fe43b28adef01c498000)]
- Proton version: 6.3-8, 7.0-1, Experimental

## I confirm:
- [ * ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ * ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

Application log attached: [steam-611500.log.gz](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/8358614/steam-611500.log.gz)

## Symptoms
- The game doesn't start
- If started with Proton 6.3-8, the game starts loading while briefly showing black screen...then returns to the desktop.
- If started with Proton 7.0-1 or Experimental, the game starts to load, then shows Quake Champions logo on black background - my screen blinks, and that is it....back to desktop
- Started past 2022-03-24 Quake Champions update, which introduced upgraded AntiCheat protection


## Reproduction
Start the game (**launch options:** `__GL_SHADER_DISK_CACHE=1 __GL_THREADED_OPTIMIZATION=1 PROTON_NO_GLSL=1 PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1 VSYNC=0 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 WINEFSYNC=1 PROTON_NO_FSYNC=0 DXVK_ASYNC=1 ENABLE_VKBASALT=1 VK_ICD_FILENAMES=/usr/share/vulkan/icd.d/nvidia_icd.json %command%` )

FYI: The same symptoms are with Quake Champions PTS.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Plain Sight
- Steam AppID of the game: 49900

## System Information
- GPU: intel hd 3000
- Video driver version: Mesa 23.3.6-1/ LLVM 17.0.6-3
- Kernel version: 6.7.9
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [report](https://gist.github.com/iodream/21f587c827e63400f174b3e3b084bd5a)
- Proton version: 1711103557 experimental-bleeding-edge-9.0-87026-20240322-pc5407b-we37b544-d28c7c0-v21008d

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

Proton log: [steam-49900.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14726674/steam-49900.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Game hangs while loading level. Also tried Proton 9 with same results.
I seem to remember the last working Proton version was Proton 5.13 but only with `protontricks xna31` applied and this no longer works(installer fails)    

## Reproduction
1. Start Plain sight
2. Click LAN only
3. Click Practice
4. Select Arena 
5. Press start

Observed result: Game freezes
Expected result: game doesn't freeze

following_discussion.discussion_body:
Hello @iodream, please keep in mind that Proton generally requires a Vulkan capable GPU for support. The `PROTON_USE_WINED3D` runtime option is not considered supported and your Sandybridge generation hardware is able to run some games with Proton more by luck than intent. Any issues with games that usually use DXVK or VKD3D-Proton with Proton need to have your issue reproduced with vanilla wine and reported to upstream wine.

Having written that, it looks like there's an issue running this game regardless of hardware.

Thank you for clarifying that. Yes i am aware that my hardware is technically unsupported but i tried to limit my reports to issues which have less to do with performance and rendering and more with general lockups and crashes, which seem to happen to other people as well.

Does this mean i should refrain from future reports?

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Rhythm Doctor
- Steam AppID of the game: 774181

## System Information
- GPU: RTX 2070
- Driver/LLVM version: nvidia 460.56
- Kernel version: 5.11.6
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/huantianad/cababa6f9e4fa55a791b98890e037933
- Proton version: 5.13-6 (also happens on experimental-5.13-20210311)

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and drag
and drop the generated `$HOME/steam-$APPID.log` into this issue report -->
[steam-774181.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6178199/steam-774181.log)
Also the game log, might be helpful: [Player.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6178200/Player.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
The game has a mechanic where the game goes out of fullscreen and moves its own window around the desktop. This mostly works fine; however, when the game attempts to move off my monitor (to create a screen wrap type of effect), it seems to be "blocked" by the edge of my monitor, and isn't allowed to be partially allowed off my screen. (I'm using KDE Plasma 5.21.3)

## Reproduction
1. Launch the game, and either enter an official level with window dance (i.e. 2-X, 4-1N).
2. Play the level, and observe the window movement when it attempts to wrap around the screen or go off the screen.
(this can also be observed with custom levels with window dance, you can reproduce it with a simple "wrap" event)

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.
3. You can retrieve a full system information report by clicking
   `Help` > `System Information` in the Steam client on your machine.
4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.
   Then paste it in a [Gist](https://gist.github.com/) and post the link in
   this issue.
5. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->

following_discussion.discussion_body: This could be a KDE specific issue; testing with a separate program, the window seems to be prevented from leaving the screen if not already off the screen

> This could be a KDE specific issue; testing with a separate program, the window seems to be prevented from leaving the screen if not already off the screen

KWin does stop that from happening unless the `wmctrl` command is used to move the window

If any window manager outright does not allow application windows to be placed in inaccessible locations, then that behavior is something to discuss with the developers of that desktop environment. Proton should not use abusive methods to override that.

@kisak-valve Makes sense, closing this issue since it's not necessarily Proton related.

@Saraistupid You seem to know more about this, have you filed an issue with KDE/KWin?

> @Saraistupid You seem to know more about this, have you filed an issue with KDE/KWin?

nope, i haven't, didn't bother tbh since i'm not affected by this

following_discussion.discussion_body: Note, playing the game using the Elden Ring Seamless Coop mod works, it's just the official game that I can't launch.

I have a weird issue where I get frame drops and stuttering, but only during the recoil animation where I take damage. All smooth gameplay right up until I take a hit, then when my character recoils, so does my computer. 

Weirdest part is the same thing happens in Dark Souls 3. It wasnt happening when I was playing in the middle of the year, I believe. 

Any suggestions?

Just throwing it out here. This game is also plagued by this awful splitlock (and bad programming from Fromsoft) that got introduced in kernel 5.19.

On my end it was so awful that the game crashed my whole system. Using the kernel boot parameter `split_lock_detect=off` solved all problems so far.

> Just throwing it out here. This game is also plagued by this awful splitlock (and bad programming from Fromsoft) that got introduced in kernel 5.19.
> 
> On my end it was so awful that the game crashed my whole system. Using the kernel boot parameter `split_lock_detect=off` solved all problems so far.

Don't think you were responding to me but this did not resolve my issue.

@lo4952 @tosirius Opting out of steam beta seems to have fixed the game+steam crash on multiplayer attempts for me. I also deleted the `~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/1245620` directory again in the process just in case. I can join colosseum pvp at least, haven't tried co op yet.

## System Info

- OS: Arch Linux
- GPU: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2070
- Driver/LLVM version: NVIDIA 525.60.11
- Kernel version: 6.1.2-x64v1-xanmod1-1
- DE: Plasma 5.26.4
- WM: KWin
---

I can confirm @imaginaryPineapple's Compability Report.

The game will launch normally without EAC.
[steam-1245620-no_eac.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10351762/steam-1245620-no_eac.log)

With, the EAC window will pop up, then close and completely die after.
[steam-1245620-eac.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10351719/steam-1245620-eac.log)

Related EAC logs:
[loader.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10351846/loader.log)
[anticheatlauncher.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10351853/anticheatlauncher.log)
[service.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10351854/service.log)


Note: The "EAC" window will only show if the `PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1` launch option is used, otherwise it will immediately crash with the following error
[steam-1245620-nowined3d-eac.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10351784/steam-1245620-nowined3d-eac.log)

Tested on `Proton Experimental`, `Proton Experimental [bleeding edge]`, `Proton 7.0-5` with the same resulting behavior



EDIT: It sounds like #6051 might be related, roughly fits the timeline of breakings as well

@SammCheese @Nicknakin 
Issue could be related to KWin or Plasma. SteamOS also uses Plasma but not sure what version they are running currently, but it seems to be working.

`PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1` in my case crashed the game as it can only work with up to directx 11. `eldenring.exe` should crash as it uses directx 12 and option disables dxvk.

EDIT: I recall around the same time as the game broke I had very nasty issues with the compositor. Trasnparency issues and random hangs.

> PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 in my case crashed the game as it can only work with up to directx 11. eldenring.exe should crash as it uses directx 12 and option disables dxvk.

The option should also disable `vkd3d-proton` and instead use wine's own `vkd3d`

> PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 in my case crashed the game as it can only work with up to directx 11. eldenring.exe should crash as it uses directx 12 and option disables dxvk.

Unfortunately that's the only way i could even get to the EAC Window :<

I could confirm that its not an issue with KWin or Plasma. I briefly switched over to XFCE where the same issue arose.

> @SammCheese @Nicknakin Issue could be related to KWin or Plasma. SteamOS also uses Plasma but not sure what version they are running currently, but it seems to be working.
> 
> `PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1` in my case crashed the game as it can only work with up to directx 11. `eldenring.exe` should crash as it uses directx 12 and option disables dxvk.
> 
> EDIT: I recall around the same time as the game broke I had very nasty issues with the compositor. Trasnparency issues and random hangs.

> Compared to my previous Windows install where I had 50-60FPS on R7 1700 and GTX 1060 6G, I get only 25-35FPS on Proton Experimental (might be due to my lower specs).

This is expected behavior on Nvidia Pascal and earlier GPUs. Performance with VKD3D is severely degraded compared to native DirectX 12 due to hardware limitations.

@SammCheese @imaginaryPineapple Any progress from either of you? I've just been running with EAC disabled but it'd be nice to get to try out the new coliseum update. I'd have thought it was a newer kernel issue but it appears that pineapple is running a 5.xx so that's probably not the issue.

I wish there was progress. I have to run with EAC disabled as well, which is really unfortunate.

> @SammCheese @imaginaryPineapple Any progress from either of you? I've just been running with EAC disabled but it'd be nice to get to try out the new coliseum update. I'd have thought it was a newer kernel issue but it appears that pineapple is running a 5.xx so that's probably not the issue.

After further investigation I discovered that none of my EAC games are working. Last time I was actively playing Apex was early December, so I suppose I'll have to backtrack and see if I've made any changes I can recall since then.

> Opting out of steam beta seems to have fixed the game+steam crash on multiplayer attempts for me. I also deleted the `~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/1245620` directory again in the process just in case. I can join colosseum pvp at least, haven't tried co op yet.

Can confirm this works, haven't tried without deleting the compatdata (proton prefix) though.
Switching to the Steam Flatpak lets me play the game multiplayer, but is not an ideal solution. Hopefully this helps @SammCheese and @imaginaryPineapple
@imaginaryPineapple Could you post a proton log with and without that variable? 🙂

Lovely lol. Also without the variable?

I put some effort and the original issue persist and the game runs only sometimes. The EAC window opens every time.
Fail:
[steam-1245620.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10446100/steam-1245620.log)
Success:
[steam-1245620.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10446149/steam-1245620.log)

Kinda strange that not only steam overlay, but even `DXVK_HUD` options only show up inside EAC window and not the game...
Also, the Bandai Namco splash loads noticeably slower with `PROTON_NO_D3D11=1`. Could be if it's wines dxgi.dll.

Sadness

> Kinda strange that not only steam overlay, but even DXVK_HUD options only show up inside EAC window and not the game...

At least the dxvk hud is expected. Elden Ring itself is d3d12 which is rendered using vkd3d-proton and not dxvk. The EAC logo splash is rendered using d3d9 for some reason and therefor uses dxvk.

I will try to install Elden Ring here when i have time to see if i experience anything similar. Will be on AMD GPU tho not Nvidia.

> Elden Ring itself is d3d12 which is rendered using vkd3d-proton and not dxvk.

Thanks for clarifying. I still try to get the overlay to work (mainly for screenshots to keep a journal), but it basically doesn't work on any game with a launcher, so yeah...

> > Elden Ring itself is d3d12 which is rendered using vkd3d-proton and not dxvk.
> 
> Thanks for clarifying. I still try to get the overlay to work (mainly for screenshots to keep a journal), but it basically doesn't work on any game with a launcher, so yeah...

The overlay should work if you can get gamescope running, I can't recall either or not you need the -e option though.

> Replying to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5613#issuecomment-1397543107

Thanks, usually gamescope halts my entire system but -e fixed it.

> > Replying to [#5613 (comment)](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5613#issuecomment-1397543107)
> 
> Thanks, usually gamescope halts my entire system but -e fixed it.

If setting -e breaks steam input you can get around that by setting the desktop input for your controller to whatever scheme you wanted elden ring to use, it sucks but it's worked for me in the past.

I haven't used gamescope, but _always_ had to use the desktop controller scheme to get gamepad support working in Elden Ring.

However, it does not work in the New Big Picture Mode, only in desktop mode or old BPM.

Does gamescope somehow fix New BPM and gamepad to work with Elden Ring?

> I haven't used gamescope, but _always_ had to use the desktop controller scheme to get gamepad support working in Elden Ring.
> 
> However, it does not work in the New Big Picture Mode, only in desktop mode or old BPM.
> 
> Does gamescope somehow fix New BPM and gamepad to work with Elden Ring?

I had the same issue at launch, gamescope was the solution to that particular problem for me. I recall the issue being it only worked without -e but -e is what allowed for the steam overlay to work, but it seems that -e works now with my controller.
Here's what my latest launch args were
`gamescope -W 2560 -H 1440 -w 2560 -h 1440 -fb  -- gamemoderun %command% `

If anyone knows why the game would be crashing after launch when running the native version of steam on arch but not while running the flatpak of steam on arch that would be awesome to know.

Thanks. I installed gamescope from the Debian repo, and tried a
similar launch option (changed the resolution, also tried with and without
-e). But it doesn't work correctly for me. It pops up a
seemingly unkillalbe window (doesn't respond to Alt+F4 or the X in the
GNOME overview, have to manually grep ps output and kill processes). I
can't see the game (see screenshot). The game did start - I could hear it -
and it was responding to my gamepad..! Would be nice if I could sort this
out, because I've been avoiding New BPM because it's broken controller
support for this game.
I'm on NVIDIA and Xorg and GNOME on Debian Testing, if any of that makes a
difference.

logo, grey bar along the bottom, and an X at the top-right. I feel like I'm
so close now, but so far!

> Replying to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5613#issuecomment-1397864506

It was a bit of a stretch because it only should have effected titles using `-steamdeck` anyway. When using `-steamdeck' Sackboy has KB/M as player one and controller as player 2 as of a recent update targeting the Steam Deck.

Gotcha.

Well, I removed the resolution options and the game now runs, fullscreen,
gamepad works, and the overlay works. But it is now stuck in 720p, for some
reason (I want 1080p). I'm gonna call it a night and plunk away at it
tomorrow.

Thanks again for the pointers towards gamescope. I briefly tried it some
months ago and didn't have good results at all (lots of freezing and such)
but it seems to have me on the right track, now.

Today's stable Steam Client update fixed the controller issue for me, meaning no longer having to copy your controller config to "Desktop Configuration." The overlay is still broken without gamescope though.

I'm trying to run Elden Ring on Pop!_OS 22.04 LTS, but it just won't work.
And I think it has something to do with EAC not being able to download from the epicgames cdn for elden ring.

here's the log I found
`/SteamLibrary/steamapps/compatdata/1245620/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/Application Data/EasyAntiCheat/773d3a68f76f4b2ebebc5b4127bbad3e/%deploymentid%/loader.log`
```
[2023.03.08-04.02.28] Loader initializing with bootstrapper version 1.3.3.
[2023.03.08-04.02.28] Loader initialized.
[2023.03.08-04.02.28] [Connection] Connecting to URL: https://modules-cdn.eac-prod.on.epicgames.com/modules/773d3a68f76f4b2ebebc5b4127bbad3e/%deploymentid%/linux32_64
[2023.03.08-04.02.28] Download Progress: 0
[2023.03.08-04.02.28] Download Progress: 0
[2023.03.08-04.02.28] Download Progress: 0
[2023.03.08-04.02.28] [Connection] Connect result: Couldn't connect to server (7) Response Code: 0 Destination IP: Unavailable
[2023.03.08-04.02.28] Connection to the Content Distribution Network failed! Curl Code: 7!
[2023.03.08-04.02.28] [EAC Callback] Code: 506. Message: 'Connection to the Content Distribution Network failed! Curl Code: 7!'.
```
The deploymentid I think is unique to every copy of Elden ring so I censored it,
and when I check this folder on the steamdeck there exists
```
easyanticheat_linux_x86_x64.eac
easyanticheat_linux_x86_x64.eac.metadata
loader.log
```
so I'm pretty sure the EAC launcher on my linux desktop is failing to download these `.eac` files and I'm getting no access to online because of it

And I can't tell why this is happening, modules-cdn.eac-prod.on.epicgames.com is up and running and reachable, and even my steamdeck can connect, 
```
[2023.03.04-15.49.09] Loader initializing with bootstrapper version 1.3.3.
[2023.03.04-15.49.09] Loader initialized.
[2023.03.04-15.49.09] [Connection] Connecting to URL: https://modules-cdn.eac-prod.on.epicgames.com/modules/773d3a68f76f4b2ebebc5b4127bbad3e/%deploymentid%/linux32_64
[2023.03.04-15.49.09] Download Progress: 0
--snip--
[2023.03.04-15.49.10] Download Progress: 0
[2023.03.04-15.49.10] [Connection] Connect result: No error (0) Response Code: 304 Destination IP: 108.159.227.101
[2023.03.04-15.49.10] No update needed.
[2023.03.04-15.49.10] Download Progress: 100
```
?!?!?

In the end I was able to copy 
```
easyanticheat_linux_x86_x64.eac
easyanticheat_linux_x86_x64.eac.metadata
```
from the deck and was able to run Elden Ring online without getting "inappropriate activity detected"!

I was able to play Elden Ring online, however for anyone in the future, you'd need to have a steamdeck to copy the files from.

Not ideal

TLDR;
inappropriate activity detected, no online
EAC on linux desktop failing curl of eac cdn
should have download `easyanticheat_linux_x86_x64.eac`
copied the files from steamdeck
online works!

## System Information
- GPU: AMD 5700 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: vulkan-radeon 22.3.6-1
- Kernel version: 6.1.12-zen1-1-zen
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/):
- https://gist.github.com/LuxLucian/f9235f1039d84b83e8402db95600321c
- Proton version: 7.0-6

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG= -->

https://mega.nz/file/xFkCELpS#O3JNKpWWTzoO8pFwB3DsYK7fbu8Cutu_wjJuwsw_Hgo

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Game crashes the session and kicks me to the login screen on X11.
On Wayland the display drivers crash (I assume), the screen freezes, goes black, and when it returns it has artifacts all over it and is unresponsive.

## Reproduction

Play the game. It's very inconsistent in how frequent it happens. Sometimes it happens as soon as I load in, other times it won't happen for a week.

The game crashes regularly on nvidia (RTX3090ti, top of the line Ryzen and 64GB RAM) as well. It happens periodically with no explanation as to why it occurs. I think idling the character in a specific spot won't trigger said eventual freeze where as switching to too many different areas in a playthrough does. As such speedrunning the game would almost guarantee running into said crash. Windows also run into these kinds of crashes, but at a far lower frequency.

Just a quick heads up, after Elden Ring continously kept crashing at startup on my openSUSE Tumbleweed NVIDIA system I finally figured out the culprit:

**FSYNC**

Turns out when I add `PROTON_NO_FSYNC=1` to my launch parameters, the game works fine for me.
*This obviously won't be useful for everyone, but maybe it works for someone else, too.*
Update 1.09 adds ray tracing support. It works on Windows. The setting is currently greyed out on Linux, at least for AMD GPU.

> Update 1.09 adds ray tracing support. It works on Windows. The setting is currently greyed out on Linux, at least for AMD GPU.


Greyed out on my NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 as well

Even when launched with `VKD3D_CONFIG=dxr11` ? and maybe optionally `PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1` on Nvidia.
AMD RADV also needs `RADV_PERFTEST=rt`

I can enable it on AMD running the game with 

`RADV_PERFTEST=rt VKD3D_CONFIG=dxr %command%`

Though I can't see much of a difference where I am on either of my characters. Except the framerate, that makes it clear it's doing something. I just can't see it.
The difference is very slim, kinda worthless feature as it is right now

> Even when launched with `VKD3D_CONFIG=dxr11` ? and maybe optionally `PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1` on Nvidia. AMD RADV also needs `RADV_PERFTEST=rt`

This works for me: `VKD3D_CONFIG=dxr11 %command% -dx12`

Still can't get ray tracing working. Adding `RADV_PERFTEST=rt VKD3D_CONFIG=dxr11` prevent me from booting the game. I can launch the game and enable ray tracing if I use `RADV_PERFTEST=rt VKD3D_CONFIG=dxr`. The game freezes after finishing loading a save, the music is playing in the background though.
My GPU is RX 6700XT, mesa 23.0.

I have another issue. Gamemode can't be enabled for Elden Ring process. It's enabled for steam/proton runtimes.
My launch option is `mangohud gamemoderun %command%`. mangohud tells me gamemode is off.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Demonicon
- Steam AppID of the game: 215630

## System Information
- GPU: <!-- e.g. RX 580 or GTX 970 --> Radeon RX 7900 XTX
- Video driver version: <!-- e.g. Mesa 18.2 or nvidia 396.54 --> Mesa 23.2.1
- Kernel version: <!-- e.g. 4.17 --> 6.6.0-rc5-273-tkg-eevdf
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Patola/5b0f3d30bfdbf276b183d32f997c8500
- Proton version: 8.0-4

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Game doesn't start

## Reproduction

Press PLAY.

[steam-215630.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12872896/steam-215630.log)

Note: previously I had a problem with various games where I couldn't start games because of amdgpu-pro and amdgpu installed at the same time, which I resolved by settings the VK_ICD_FILENAMES variable to point only to amdgpu. I find it odd that proton.log has these lines:
```
info:  Required Vulkan extension VK_KHR_surface not supported
147363.841:0124:0128:trace:seh:raise (22)
terminate called after throwing an instance of 'dxvk::DxvkError'
```
even though `vulkaninfo` tells me that my GPU supports this vulkan extension.

following_discussion.discussion_body: Hello @Patola, please copy the contents of Steam Runtime Diagnostics from Steam (`Steam` -> `Help` -> `Steam Runtime Diagnostics`) and put it in a [gist](https://gist.github.com/), then include a link to the gist in this issue report.
Sorry for the delay, here it is: https://gist.github.com/Patola/225fdf5f50c3b227f33092a19ee18b1a

Thanks, this looks like the section to focus on https://gist.github.com/Patola/225fdf5f50c3b227f33092a19ee18b1a#file-steam-runtime-diagnostics-patola-txt-L6518-L6528. As a quick test, can you go to the per-game properties of `Steam Linux Runtime 3.0 (sniper)` in your Steam library, and switch to the `client_beta` beta branch and test if that has any effect?

Found the Steam Linux Runtime 3.0 (sniper), on the properties windows, betas tab, changed it to client_beta, waited for it to update and ran Demonicon again. Same thing, the game doesn't start, sending the proton log.

[steam-215630.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12909789/steam-215630.log)

On my Steam Runtime Diagnostics, what was specially relevant on the part you marked? The double-free part is sort of a driver bug or something?

I've completely removed amdgpu-pro from my system, unset VK_ICD_FILENAMES (removed from /etc/environment) and rebooted. Game still does not start, generated a new gist: https://gist.github.com/Patola/5de85bdf2523ab4a1b89279e56f78fac

This is looking more like a your-computer-specific issue than a game-specific issue. Do other Vulkan or DXVK games work? It's often useful to try other games as a way to check whether something is game-specific, especially free-to-play games which are equally accessible to all users. Some good ones to try here might be:

* Ghostrunner (free demo) via Proton 8.0 - uses DXVK in the sniper container
* Counter-Strike 2 - free, native Linux game, runs in the sniper container
* Dota 2 - technologically similar to CS2

I see you have MangoHud installed. Conveniently, that will tell you whether a game is using DXVK.

amdgpu-pro is known to be problematic, so it's good to have removed it completely to rule out it being part of the problem.

I also notice that you have something called `VK_LAYER_ALVR_capture` installed, which is unusual. It might be worthwhile to make sure that's disabled during testing.

>             "free(): double free detected in tcache 2",
>            "timeout: the monitored command dumped core"

This specific issue was (probably) a bug in the diagnostic tool that is generating the report, nothing to do with your specific driver. This specific issue is fixed in the `client_beta`, which is part of why Kisak asked you to try that.

Looking at your latest report, the "double free" bug is fixed for sniper there, as expected (it's still present for soldier, presumably because you didn't switch soldier to its equivalent beta).

Is it intentional that you have the Nvidia proprietary driver installed on a machine that doesn't appear to have an Nvidia GPU? That seems odd to me. If DXVK is somehow selecting that driver, then that could be causing problems for you. DXVK has its own logic for choosing which Vulkan rendering device to use, and does not necessarily use devices in the order in which they are provided to it by lower layers.

Non-DXVK Vulkan seems to be working fine in your sniper container: both the 32- and 64-bit versions of the diagnostic tool (which is a simple Vulkan program that draws one triangle) can render successfully either on your GPU or in software.

*Outside* the sniper container, 32-bit Vulkan is fine, but the diagnostic tool is finding that something is not right with 64-bit Vulkan: it has taken longer than a few seconds to start up the renderer:

> ```
>       "graphics-details" : {
>         "x11/vulkan" : {
>           "renderer" : null,
>           "version" : null,
>           "issues" : [
>             "cannot-load",
>             "timeout",
>             "cannot-draw"
>           ],
>           "exit-status" : 124,
> ```

> This is looking more like a your-computer-specific issue than a game-specific issue. Do other Vulkan or DXVK games work? It's often useful to try other games as a way to check whether something is game-specific, especially free-to-play games which are equally accessible to all users. Some good ones to try here might be:
> 
>     * Ghostrunner (free demo) via Proton 8.0 - uses DXVK in the sniper container
> 
>     * Counter-Strike 2 - free, native Linux game, runs in the sniper container
> 
>     * Dota 2 - technologically similar to CS2

Just to make things clear, after these tests I reinstalled the amdgpu-pro packages (making the default be the vk_radv via /etc/environment) because these are the only packages that provide `amdgpu_dri.so`. And also, without the amdgpu-pro packages installed, this steam diagnostic that I was asked to run had some odd error messages like in x11/vdpau libGL saying that it can't open .drirc and renderer being null, and graphics-details-x11/vulkan with null renderer (with amdgpu-pro installed (but not being the default) this section has "AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX (RADV GFX1100)" as renderer and version is "Mesa 23.2.1-arch1.2" as expected).

All of these games you mentioned worked without a hitch, just tested them. In fact on this machine I usually run about 10 different Steam games each and every day, it's the odd ones that don't run that I report. So it's got an excellent compatibility track record. I've also got other machines that work as control, my nvidia rtx 3070 laptop (where Demonicon started the launcher, but got stuck anyway -- I was just going to add that), my Steam Deck and my wife's PC with Ubuntu and RTX 2070 Super (that she's upgrading to a RX 6800 XT). Please let me know if you want proton logs and machine details from each of them and I will run the game there.

> I see you have MangoHud installed. Conveniently, that will tell you whether a game is using DXVK.

It tells when a screen appear, which is not the case for this game. Maybe I can make it show something by turning on logging?

> amdgpu-pro is known to be problematic, so it's good to have removed it completely to rule out it being part of the problem.

Unfortunately due to the oddities I mentioned I prefer to have it installed. There are a few games that I prefer to run with amdgpu-pro too, although this thing that happened that I had to specify RADV instead is a recent development, some arch update might have changed some default or something.

> I also notice that you have something called `VK_LAYER_ALVR_capture` installed, which is unusual. It might be worthwhile to make sure that's disabled during testing.

ALVR is a steamvr streaming software for the Oculus Quest 2 and Pico VR headsets. I have it installed there but inactive, apparently this comes from /usr/share/vulkan/explicit_layer.d/alvr_x86_64.json, but as I only use this machine with the Valve Index I can remove it to test, of course.

> > ```
> >         "free(): double free detected in tcache 2",
> >        "timeout: the monitored command dumped core"
> > ```
> 
> This specific issue was (probably) a bug in the diagnostic tool that is generating the report, nothing to do with your specific driver. This specific issue is fixed in the `client_beta`, which is part of why Kisak asked you to try that.

Ah, understood.

> Looking at your latest report, the "double free" bug is fixed for sniper there, as expected (it's still present for soldier, presumably because you didn't switch soldier to its equivalent beta).

Should I switch soldier to beta? Will it help in this case, or just other general cases?

> Is it intentional that you have the Nvidia proprietary driver installed on a machine that doesn't appear to have an Nvidia GPU? That seems odd to me. If DXVK is somehow selecting that driver, then that could be causing problems for you. DXVK has its own logic for choosing which Vulkan rendering device to use, and does not necessarily use devices in the order in which they are provided to it by lower layers.

I didn't like this but it's also recent, ffmpeg-full-git required me to have cuda installed which by its turns pulls nvidia-utils and opencl-nvidia. Having said that I instead installed `ffmpeg-amd-full-git` and I was then able to remove these pesky three packages.

> Non-DXVK Vulkan seems to be working fine in your sniper container: both the 32- and 64-bit versions of the diagnostic tool (which is a simple Vulkan program that draws one triangle) can render successfully either on your GPU or in software.
> 
> _Outside_ the sniper container, 32-bit Vulkan is fine, but the diagnostic tool is finding that something is not right with 64-bit Vulkan: it has taken longer than a few seconds to start up the renderer:
> 
> > ```
> >       "graphics-details" : {
> >         "x11/vulkan" : {
> >           "renderer" : null,
> >           "version" : null,
> >           "issues" : [
> >             "cannot-load",
> >             "timeout",
> >             "cannot-draw"
> >           ],
> >           "exit-status" : 124,
> > ```

I don't know what to make of it. However, games that are supposed to work via radv do it ok. Starfield, for example, would not work via amdgpu-pro, but works with RADV, and this is still the case with amdgpu-pro installed.

[steamdiag-3.json](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12919693/steamdiag-3.json)
[steamdiag-1.json](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12919694/steamdiag-1.json)
[steamdiag-2.json](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12919695/steamdiag-2.json)
Maybe this helps figuring out what's going on? steamdiag-1 is pior to uninstalling amdgpu-pro (although I also uninstalled `amdgpu-core-meta` which I can't find again, there's been some renaming of packages), steamdiag-2 is after having uninstalled amdgpu-pro, and steamdiag-3 is after I have reinstalled amdgpu-pro (`paru -S amdgpu-pro-oglp amf-amdgpu-pro lib32-amdgpu-pro-oglp lib32-vulkan-amdgpu-pro  vulkan-amdgpu-pro`). Please understand that I'm not asking for help to get my machine "fixed", I am doing that because I think it might be a point of contention for this case and others, and it's important to know that if there's an issue, it's better for everyone to know where it comes from.

Ok, I just tested Demonicon on my gaming laptop, running Arch Linux with proprietary nvidia drivers for the Mobile RTX 3070 GPU.
system information of the laptop -- https://gist.github.com/Patola/59b59f9e71a2f085a42d3d574bf57810
systeam diagnostics of the laptop -- https://gist.github.com/Patola/74667b8a30c91c71d5f187ed8330c046

So, what happens on this case? I press play and this window appears:
![image](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/2991620/d79035b3-ca5a-408c-b3a5-0fcdd49c0aeb)
I then press "install update" and the window disappears and the game keeps running forever with nothing happening (waited 13h then pressed stop on Steam).
This is the proton log: 
[steam-215630.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12920626/steam-215630.log)

So... it's quite different from what is happening on my desktop, where even this initial launcher screen does not appear.

...ok, this is creepy. Demonicon just started working in that gaming laptop with NVIDIA. It goes to a launcher first, then I can press a button to play the game. First time it didn't work but now it does, and apparently everything is working perfectly now on that laptop. I had no updates in between the tests, I hadn't even rebooted the laptop. Sending an updated proton log if that helps comparing.
[steam-215630.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12920735/steam-215630.log)
The game still doesn't work on my AMD desktop, though.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Stars Reach Playtest
- Steam AppID of the game: 3361750

## System Information
- GPU: GTX 1060 6G
- Video driver version: 550.144.03
- Kernel version: 6.13.1-2-MANJARO
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/ChrisClems/d8a052597718ed43a389b36d79c8fdb0
- Proton version: 9.0-4

## I confirm:
- [ X ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ X ] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-3361750.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19032485/steam-3361750.log)

## Symptoms

On Proton 9, game crashes to desktop as soon as the game world loads. There is what appears to be a Unity crash report dialog that pops up momentarily until the game closes. Proton <9 hangs at "Logging in..." and never loads the character select menu.

## Reproduction

Happens predictably on Proton 9, Proton GE 9, and Proton Experimental. Happens on both the Dx and Vulcan renderers.

In the game Discord I managed to find a report from someone who is running it with no issues. They claim to have not forced any specific Proton version. They were running CachyOS with the current kernel. Open source AMD drivers.  Running under Wayland and KDE Plasma.

following_discussion.discussion_body: Hello @ChrisClems, `wine: Unhandled illegal instruction at address 00006FFFFB0962FC (thread 018c), starting debugger...` hints that the game is trying to use a CPU feature that your Sandy Bridge generation does not have, but it's not immediately clear what specific feature that is.

> Hello [@ChrisClems](https://github.com/ChrisClems), `wine: Unhandled illegal instruction at address 00006FFFFB0962FC (thread 018c), starting debugger...` hints that the game is trying to use a CPU feature that your Sandy Bridge generation does not have, but it's not immediately clear what specific feature that is.

Interesting. I don't think I ever would have thought of that. Can you recommend any ways in which I might be able to investigate further?
It looks like this was an unintended AVX2 dependency which has since been patched out. Game is running fine via Proton9+ currently.

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Marvel's Midnight Suns
- Steam AppID of the game: 368260

## System Information
- Steam Deck 
- Steam OS Stable, Latest Version
- Proton version: 7.0.5, Experimental

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.


## Symptoms 
> **Crashes on end of day**.
The game runs on a day by day cycle where after every mission you go to bed and change days. Whenever you try to go to sleep, it hard crashes freezing the deck and requiring power cycling.

> **Crashes on loading saves**

> **Crashes on manually saving**

>**Save file paths move around preventing syncing**
Game moves save file paths move around constantly for seemingly no reason when tweaks are made.
HOWEVER I BELIEVE THIS IS A DEV PROBLEM. I just mention it to be thorough.


--
People are reporting contradictory fixes which seem to work for some including forcing dx11 or pining the clocks at 1400mhz, but in general, for most, the game is too unstable to be playable. It's a shame since it works fine in between crashes. It's a stability problem, not so much a performance one, though its not great either.



## Reproduction
End of day crash: Finish a mission and send the player character to sleep
Crash on loading/saving: Can happen when doing either. Inconsistent

-->

following_discussion.discussion_body:
An additional issue is cloud saves. What happens is that your game saves go into a folder called `Saved_Steam_[your steam userid]` but the folder that gets cloud saved is just `Saved_Steam_`

It's fixable by just deleting `Saved_Steam_` and making a symlink from `Saved_Steam_[userid]` to `Saved_Steam_` 

So my theory is that Steamworks (maybe) is failing to get the userid at some point.

## System Information
- GPU:  AMD Radeon 6900XT
- Driver/LLVM version: [Mesa 23.0-git](https://gitlab.freedesktop.org/mesa/mesa/-/commit/05c17eae2b96e44c01c8b5731c5c63d23307d8aa)/15.0.6
- Kernel version: 6.2-rc2
- Link to full system information report as [gist](https://gist.github.com/NTMan/33cca4906328793206218192820779ec)
- Proton version: 1673001906 experimental-bleeding-edge-7.0-32554-20230106-p2bf76f-w4e4fde-dfd34db-v5d6dca

attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
After launching the game nothing happens. Likely the game hangs. I've been waiting for an hour for something to happen. (I only can stop the game with STOP button in steam client)

![Screenshot from 2023-01-06 22-25-09](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/200750/211064973-64d33221-2170-4a77-9d3f-1ab107d2f06c.png)

Always

following_discussion.discussion_body:
## System Information
- GPU: RTX 3070<!-- e.g. RX 580 or GTX 970 -->
- Driver/LLVM version: 495.46<!-- e.g. Mesa 18.2/7.0.0 or nvidia 396.54 -->
- Kernel version: 5.4.0-94-generic<!-- e.g. 4.17 -->
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/Corben78/5d071b9a80bbfd791b906f7098683314)
- Proton version: 4.2-9
- Proton version: 6.3-8c

## I confirm:
- [x] that I have found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->
[steam-12330-proton-4.2-9.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7885616/steam-12330-proton-4.2-9.log)
[steam-12330-proton-6.3-8c.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7885617/steam-12330-proton-6.3-8c.log) using `PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1`
[steam-12330-proton-6.3-8c-dvxk.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7888844/steam-12330-proton-6.3-8c-dvxk.log) using DXVK
[steam-12330.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7888862/steam-12330.log) without any override

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Game does start and seems playable with Proton 4.2-9
Game does start with newer versions of Proton than 4.2-9 (4.11-13+) but crashes immediately after the very first logo intro video when the screen says Darkstar One loading.
Game does start with Proton 4.11-13+ when disabling DXVK via `PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1` and seems playable for a bit but crashes randomly after either intro video or in the first tutorial mission.

## Reproduction

Game seems to work:
1. install game
2. set Proton version to 4.2-9
3. set launch options to `WINEDLLOVERRIDES="iphlpapi=b" %command%` (according to [PCGamingWiki](https://www.pcgamingwiki.com/wiki/Darkstar_One#Issues_fixed) the shipped iphlpapi.dll is outdated and causes game crashes on higher resolutions, e.g. full HD. edit: seems to work without the override)
4. after watching the videos, accept tutorial mission from terminal, leave station, fly in circles, there are some stutters which don't appear in later versions (at least not in 6.3-8c)

Game starts with disabling DXVK, but crashes:
1. install game
2. set Proton to anything after 4.2-9 (here 6.3-8c, but tested with 4.11-13 onward as well, same issue)
3. set launch options to `PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 WINEDLLOVERRIDES="iphlpapi=b" %command%`
4. skipping videos might already cause a crash, watching them allows to start the first tutorial mission, game crashes after a while during the first tutorial (seems to crash always at the same point)

## Additional Information

Tests with Proton 6.3-8c:
- setting prefix to win7
- `PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=0`
- `PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1`
- `PROTON_OLD_GL_STRING=1`
- `WINEDLLOVERRIDES="iphlpapi=b,msvcr71=b"` (as msvcr71.dll is listed in the proton logfile with the path from the game's folder)
- upgrading from 4.2-9 to 6.3-8c and adding `PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1`

none of the above made a difference, the game always crashed after a while.

Problematic line from the Proton 6.3-8c log file might to be:
`45533.092:0108:010c:err:seh:dispatch_exception Fatal EXCEPTION_WINE_CXX_EXCEPTION exception (code=e06d7363) raised`

## I confirm:

[steam-1182110.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/9024526/steam-1182110.log)
me.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

## Symptoms
Game crashes when opening the "pause" menu or interact with the in-game computer after seemingly random actions

## Reproduction
This seems to happen randomly, but has something to do with opening the "pause" menu by pressing escape, or by exiting the in-game computer by pressing escape

following_discussion.discussion_body: Just for added information, this game was built with unity and ships with runtime `2020.3.35f1` as of this comment
